lintExempt.rationale: Re-affirmed 2026-04-15 (task #22 audit, post-v0.20.0 / post-PR #208). These phase JSONs are shipped historical execution records of PH-01 through PH-04 work; no automated runtime consumer exists (verified: zero grep hits in `server/**`, zero references in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, zero usage in test suites, ac-lint is a pure function library that only runs on caller-supplied plans). The only voluntary consumer is `forge_lint_refresh planPath=<this-file>`, which is an audit-time operation against a specific path and would only re-surface pre-existing F-55/F-56 violations describing already-shipped work. Live drift in newly authored plans is prevented by `npm run lint` (CI-enforced via PR #208) and per-call ac-lint, not by retroactive cleanup of historical records. Original C1-bootstrap "we'll unwind later" framing superseded — exemption retained as the correct end-state for shipped artifacts. Unwind via `grep -l 2026-04-13-c1-bootstrap .ai-workspace/plans/*.json` only if a future runtime consumer is added that would load these files automatically.

stories.title: Implement checkBudget as a pure function over tagged-union prior records (REQ-06, NFR-C04, NFR-C09)
stories.description: Adds checkBudget(priorRecords: ReadonlyArray<PrimaryRecord | GeneratorRecord>, budgetUsd: number | undefined) to server/lib/coordinator.ts. Sums estimatedCostUsd across primary records only via explicit .filter(r => r.source === 'primary') — generator records have no cost field. Returns {usedUsd, budgetUsd, remainingUsd, incompleteData, warningLevel}. At ≥80% of budgetUsd, warningLevel is 'approaching'; at ≥100%, 'exceeded'. When budgetUsd is undefined, warningLevel is 'none' and remainingUsd is null. When any primary record has null/missing estimatedCostUsd, that record is excluded from the sum and incompleteData is set to true (the caller's logging path emits the P45 warning — checkBudget is pure, no logging or RunContext mutation). When warningLevel === 'exceeded', the brief's recommendation includes 'complete current story then stop' guidance; the coordinator never throws or aborts (advisory only per NFR-C04).

acceptanceCriteria.description: checkBudget exported with the (priorRecords, budgetUsd) signature (no ctx parameter)
At 79% of budget → warningLevel 'none'; at 80% → 'approaching'; at 100% → 'exceeded'
When budgetUsd is undefined → warningLevel 'none' and remainingUsd null (no-op)
Generator records (source === 'generator') are excluded from the sum
Primary record with null estimatedCostUsd → that record is excluded AND incompleteData is true (NFR-C09 — never silent $0)
checkBudget never throws when warningLevel is 'exceeded' — the brief is returned and the coordinator continues (NFR-C04)
checkBudget is pure: takes no RunContext, no logger; verified by signature inspection

stories.title: Implement checkTimeBudget as a pure wall-clock advisory (REQ-07)
stories.description: Adds checkTimeBudget(startTimeMs: number | undefined, maxTimeMs: number | undefined): {elapsedMs, maxTimeMs, warningLevel} to server/lib/coordinator.ts. warningLevel ∈ {'none', 'approaching', 'exceeded', 'unknown'}. At ≥80% of maxTimeMs, 'approaching'; at ≥100%, 'exceeded'. When startTimeMs is missing/undefined, elapsedMs is null and warningLevel is 'unknown' (NOT 'none' — the distinction between 'we don't know' and 'no limit set' is preserved). When maxTimeMs is missing/undefined, warningLevel is 'none' (no-op). Pure computation — never throws, never mutates external state. Documented known limitation: Date.now() is susceptible to clock jumps (NTP, DST, manual adjustment); v1 does not address this.

acceptanceCriteria.description: checkTimeBudget exported with the (startTimeMs, maxTimeMs) signature
At 80% elapsed → warningLevel 'approaching'; at 100% → 'exceeded'
Missing startTimeMs → elapsedMs null, warningLevel 'unknown' (NOT 'none')
Missing maxTimeMs → warningLevel 'none' (no-op)
checkTimeBudget never throws — pure computation

stories.title: INCONCLUSIVE flows through retry counter; transitive dep-failed propagation only on terminal-failed roots (REQ-08)
stories.description: Verifies and (where missing) wires the v1.1 INCONCLUSIVE handling: an INCONCLUSIVE eval increments retryCount the same way FAIL does (REQ-04 already counts both). The story re-enters ready-for-retry if retryCount < 3 with priorEvalReport populated, or becomes failed if retryCount >= 3. Story-level isolation is preserved (unrelated ready/ready-for-retry/pending stories remain dispatchable), but phase-brief-level forward-progress is explicitly NOT preserved (any failed or dep-failed → brief.status === 'needs-replan' per REQ-05 rule 3 — this is the explicit v1.1 behavior change). Transitive dep-failed propagation applies ONLY when the root story becomes terminally failed; a dep is NOT dep-failed merely because an upstream is ready-for-retry (it waits as 'pending' until the retry resolves PASS or exhausts). Flaky-eval compensation is explicitly rejected — the remediation is to fix the eval tool upstream (§7).

acceptanceCriteria.description: INCONCLUSIVE record + retryCount < 3 → status 'ready-for-retry' (NOT a terminal blocking state)
Mixed budget exhaustion: 1 FAIL + 1 INCONCLUSIVE + 1 FAIL → retryCount === 3, status 'failed'
Three consecutive INCONCLUSIVE records → retryCount === 3, status 'failed' (retry cap is about budget, not fault attribution)
Story whose dependency is ready-for-retry remains 'pending' (NOT 'dep-failed' — dep-failed only triggers on terminal failed roots)
When every story is failed or dep-failed → brief.status 'needs-replan' (REQ-05 rule 3 instance)

stories.title: Implement recoverState as a pure function over the reconciled view (REQ-09, NFR-C03)
stories.description: Adds recoverState(plan, projectPath) to server/lib/coordinator.ts. Pure function — reads readRunRecords(), filters source === 'primary', filters by storyId match against current-plan stories, returns the reconstructed status map. Composition: reconcileState (PH-03 US-05) runs FIRST inside assessPhase (before recoverState) — keep orphan-filter and new-story-marking logic OUT of recoverState. Operates on the already-reconciled view: most-recent PASS → done; otherwise re-derive retryCount from non-PASS primary records and let the 6-state precedence in assessPhase assign the final status. Populate priorEvalReport from the most recent non-PASS record's embedded evalReport when status is ready-for-retry or failed. no-record → pass-through, no-storyId → skip. Running assessPhase twice in a row on the same inputs (no intervening writes) returns structurally identical CoordinateResult objects.

acceptanceCriteria.description: recoverState exported and pure (no RunContext parameter, no logger parameter)
acceptanceCriteria.command: grep -nE 'export.*function recoverState' server/lib/coordinator.ts && ! grep -nE 'export.*function recoverState\([^)]*RunContext' server/lib/coordinator.ts

acceptanceCriteria.description: Crash-safe re-run: writing a partial set of RunRecords (simulated mid-run kill) and invoking assessPhase returns a CoordinateResult structurally equal to a run where all records were written atomically
Idempotency: assessPhase called twice in a row on same inputs returns deeply-equal CoordinateResults (excluding timestamps)
no-storyId record → skipped without throwing
priorEvalReport on a 'failed' story is populated from the most recent non-PASS record's embedded evalReport
There is no persistent coordinator state file on disk — verified by listing .forge/ subdirs after a run
